excerpt: Do not befriend the drunkard, for he is cursed; nor the seller of wine… They are called enemies of God in the heavens
Whoever hires a worker and compels him to labor, then withholds his full wages, Allah shall render his deeds void, and I shall be his adversary on the Day of Resurrection
O Living One, O Self-Subsisting One… By Thy mercy do I seek aid
When Allah created Paradise, He asked it, For whom have I created you… whoever opposes his base desires—Paradise becomes his dwelling
When you give alms, give from what is good to you… better than a thousand dinars given in charity from abundance
When you have completed your ablution, take water and wipe it upon your throat… Allah shall forgive him all his sins, great and small
The religion of one who consumes what is lawful becomes pure; his heart grows tender; his eyes flow with the fear of God
Always speak the truth; let no falsehood ever leave your mouth… Cultivate noble character and shun base conduct
Speak no lies, for a lie blackens the face… Through truthfulness a man's face becomes radiant and he is written righteous in God's sight
When the eyes begin to behold the desires of the soul, the heart becomes blind forever after
source_work: Aqwāl Zarrīn / Golden Sayings attributed to Islamic sages

excerpt: Strive to be in the world, not of the world. The boat floats well so long as it remains in the water; but when water enters the boat, it sinks
Three things keep a person distant from God: estimating highly one's own deeds, forgetting one's sins, and considering oneself the best

Allah the Almighty bestows five things along with five others: with gratitude, abundance of wealth; with prayer, answered supplication; with forgiveness, pardon; with charity, acceptance; and with mercy, compassion
Whatever you bring from the market for children, give first to the daughter, then to the son
Four deeds rank among the most difficult: forgiving sins when angry; showing generosity in poverty; maintaining solitude in spiritual recollection; and speaking truth in times of fear or desire
Three addictions are extremely swift—the first two degenerate, the last progressive: the addiction to wealth, the addiction to beauty, and the addiction to knowledge
Eight things do not come to an end: the eye's sight through sleep, the earth from rain, woman from man, the world from knowledge…
